events.comment:
Three.js reduces the far plane to match the light range.
So, we need to set it again or it will stay at the lowest value the range took.
We do the min() on our side to in order:
- to avoid a side effect when Three.js is upgraded
- because Three.js keeps the old value in cache and doesn't apply it back unless we set the lower value explicitly ourselves.

eventsFunctions.name: ApplyShadowCameraFarPlane
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply shadow camera far plane of _PARAM0_ to _PARAM1_

eventsFunctions.description: the shadow bias of the light. Use this to avoid "shadow acne" due to depth buffer precision. Choose a value small enough like 0.001 to avoid creating distance between shadows and objects but not too small to avoid shadow glitches on low/medium quality. This value is used for high quality, and multiplied by 2 for medium quality and 4 for low quality.
eventsFunctions.name: ShadowBias
ApplyShadowBias
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply shadow bias of _PARAM0_ to _PARAM1_ for a _PARAM2_ quality and cone angle _PARAM3_

events.comment:
Three.js reduces the far plane to match the light range.
So, we need to set it again or it will stay at the lowest value the range took.
We do the min() on our side to in order:
- to avoid a side effect when Three.js is upgraded
- because Three.js keeps the old value in cache and doesn't apply it back unless we set the lower value explicitly ourselves.
